titleStmt.title: Letter from Emily Weddall (Emhin Ní Uadall) to Nancy O'Rahilly, c. May 1916.
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Emily Weddall (Emhin Ní Uadall)

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter of condolence to Nancy O'Rahilly (1875-1961). The writer expresses sympathy with Mrs. O'Rahilly on the death of her husband - she will be calling on her when in Dublin.Nancy O'Rahilly was a member of the Provisional Council of Cumann na mBan at the founding in 1914 and the wife of Michael Joseph O'Rahilly, self-described as 'The' O'Rahilly (1875-1916). The O'Rahilly fought in the Easter Rising and was shot in Parnell/Moore Street on 28 April 1916 and died some time later. Nancy was pregnant at the time of her husband's death – the child was born three months later. The author is Emily Weddall (1867-1952), founder of Scoil Acla in Achill, County Mayo.

correspAction.placeName: Páirc na Carraige, Acaill, Co Mhuigheó, Ireland.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Dear Madam O'Rahilly, I cannot pass through
Dublin without calling to enquire
for you and to pay you my respects.
I cannot tell you how deeply we
feel your great loss - our loss and
Ireland's. You have made a great
sacrifice for us and we are profoundly
grateful to you. Yours with deep reverence, (E.M. Weddall).
Emhin NÃ­ Uadall. PÃ¡irc na Carraige Acaill Co. MaigeÃ³ Achill Island
